label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's go through the story step by step. The author and her family are on their way to California when they stop for lunch. A man, described as having a dirty face, opens the door for them and greets them. The daughters comment on his smell, so the mom explains to look beyond the dirt and sets an example by being polite. Later, when the author goes back to get the car-sick pills, the same man is opening the door again. She says a loud "thank you." Then she talks to him, learns he can't go inside unless he buys food, and decides to order extra for him. She tells the family and they understand. The man is grateful, and the mom hugs him. The lesson is about showing gratitude and teaching the children by example. Now the question is why she said "thank you" to the doorman. Let me check the options: A) She wanted to give the children a lesson of gratitude. B) She showed her real love for him. C) She wanted to give the doorman a hand. D) She just showed her polite manner. In the story, she mentions wanting to set a good example for her children. When she says thank you, it's part of teaching them to be grateful. The main purpose here is to teach the children. Later actions, like adding food, reinforce that. The final reflection says she chose to show gratitude and teach by
